msg: Shepherd live, primed-and-holding. Confirmed live gate: WS-33 Status=On hold, 8 WIs linked. Inbox watcher armed (persistent). Holding for release directive to refine Captured slice to DoR and/or begin execution. Cosmo-Stage verdict watcher deferred until release.

ACK ORION directive. Both watchers armed persistent: inbox (release/rulings) + Cosmo-Stage WS-33 (verdicts/transitions, baselined 6 Captured-Unset / WI-1184 Refining / WI-1204 Ready, all Active). PRIME-AND-HOLD confirmed; no execution-class dispatch. Holding for release directive (likely: refine 6 Captured to DoR first).

CHECKPOINT: slice DoR + sequenced. 6 Captured refined to Ready (Cosmo re-read + Stage-watcher confirmed); WI-1204 Ready; WI-1184 held Refining (repro-blocked). Order 100 WI-1204 / 200 WI-1208 / 300 WI-1212 / 400 WI-1210 / 500 WI-1209 / 600 WI-1248 / 700 WI-1142 / 800 WI-1184. KEY ADJUDICATIONS (evidence over advisory): (1) NO Work Package — 1208/1209/1210 touch DISJOINT files, kept as 3 separate parallel Items; (2) WI-1208 likely ALREADY FIXED (handleBack already stays in-shell) -> verify-first, deliverable is a regression test; (3) WI-1209 localize fix, do not touch shared goBackOrReplace (blast radius); (4) WI-1210 copy default is correctness-anchored (non-blocking, operator may tweak in review); (5) WI-1142 test-heavy -> branch not worktree. No blocking escalation. WI-1184 parked last (blocks nothing); will attempt staging repro via qa executor when reached, escalate blocked only if unobtainable. Proceeding straight into execution wave now (builders per order); will not wait on this line. FLAG (cross-lane, ORION may capture/fix): cosmo:triage triage.ts judge auto-detect uses Unix which -> ENOENT crash on Windows; workaround --judge-provider claude per call.

Reviewer bounced WI-1204 (rework) on a MECHANICAL DoD gate, not a code defect: a Type=Bug requires the Acceptance-Criteria text to declare a red-green-revert regression guard clause naming what the guard asserts. The actual guard was built + verified; only the AC wording was under-specified. This is SYSTEMIC — the refine pass wrote the same under-specified AC for every Bug in the slice (WI-1204/1208/1209/1210), so 1210/1209 would bounce identically. Swept: appended a red-green-revert clause (describing each WI real assertion) to the AC of all four Bug WIs, and re-completing WI-1204 against the conformant AC (code already merged, PR #1825). CROSS-LANE LEARNING (ORION may propagate like the which-bug fix): the refine step Bug-AC template must include a red-green-revert guard clause or the review DoD rejects at complete. Non-blocking; handled within mandate.

ACK muxnav-inbox-007 — already in flight, matches your directive. General executor dispatched to batch-append the red-green-revert guard clause to the AC of ALL four Type=Bug WIs in the slice (WI-1204/1208/1209/1210), each naming its specific assertion, then re-complete WI-1204 (--fixed-in 267b743f6). Your WI-1208 caveat resolved: WI-1208 does NOT lack a guard — a pre-existing, verified-passing regression test covers it (pick-book/[subjectId].test.tsx:417-429, landed with the fix in 75ace69609); its AC clause cites that. So this is wording-only across all four, NO real guard gap in the slice. Good to know it maps to tracked WI-1266.

WI-1204 CLOSED (AC red-green amendment cleared the DoD — good). But WI-1208 needs a ruling: it is verified ALREADY-FIXED (no new work; pre-existing passing test pick-book/[subjectId].test.tsx:417-429), and /cosmo:qa has double-bounced it on a genuine already-fixed-vs-DoD gap. Two reviewer objections: (1) completion-summary cited short file paths not resolvable from repo root — I will fix (prefix apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/...); (2) DoD requires a Fixed In commit whose required checks are GREEN, but an already-fixed WI has NO new landing PR and the historical fix commit 75ace69609 shows a FAILED CI run. QUESTION (program-level, same class as WI-1266): what is the sanctioned disposition for an already-fixed item? Options I see: (a) cite current green main HEAD as Fixed In (fix IS present+green on main, but provenance-inaccurate for what Fixed In means); (b) reviewer closes via a not-reproducible/already-fixed Resolution that bypasses the green-landing-commit DoD; (c) build a token net-new PR (e.g. strengthen the existing test) so there is a real green landing commit. I lean (b) as the honest path but will not invent misleading provenance without your ruling. WI-1208 blocks nothing — parked in Executing; continuing the rest of the slice.

WI-1208 re-completed to Reviewing per your ruling (honest Fixed In=75ace69609; resolution class recorded in prose since no flag exists). Two tooling-gap specifics for your gap-WI: (1) /cosmo:execute complete has NO Resolution/not-reproducible flag — execute.ts exposes only a ci-status positional (success/green/escalated) + --fixed-in, so an already-fixed close cannot be signaled structurally, only in prose; the reviewer must infer it. (2) complete APPENDS the completion summary (never replaces), so the earlier bounced summary with the wrong short paths still sits ABOVE the corrected one — if /cosmo:qa re-scans the whole page body it may re-flag those stale paths (append-not-replace deadlock, finalization-runbook s8). Neither is fixable from the executor side without an unauthorized page-body replace. If WI-1208 bounces a 3rd time on either limit, I park it per your instruction (blocks nothing). FYI: complete best-effort draft-PR step cross-referenced a pre-existing UNRELATED open PR #1832 (four-strands-language-planner) — builder did NOT create or touch it. Also: the create-subject.tsx path I briefed was wrong (real: apps/mobile/src/app/create-subject.tsx, top-level app/, not under (app)/); builder verified + used the correct path.

WI-1248 (P3 Hygiene, Button sweep / WI-1081 tail) is MIS-REFINED — needs a scope ruling. GATE-0 recon evidence: its AC claims an rg command that does NOT exist; real remaining scope is ~94-97 files across ~9 screen-areas and is NOT a uniform mechanical transform — 2 spot-checked files alone yield 5 judgment classes (new danger-styled destructive sites beyond the AC named 2; non-standard py-2.5 sizing Button cannot express; rounded-full shape mismatches; false-positive non-button Pressables; flex-1/layout constraints Button API cannot express — it takes no style/className override). Prerequisite finding: Button.tsx lacks a style/className override AND a danger variant, which several sites need. My earlier single-PR ruling was WRONG (builder evidence overrides it); builder stood down, no code written, claim released. DECISION NEEDED (scope/completion): (A) decompose WI-1248 into a WP with ~9 screen-area children + run now as rolling background — extends the lane materially; (B) decompose + DEFER to a dedicated future design-system pass, close the other 7 WIs now, track WI-1248 WP separately; (C) do only the genuinely-uniform true-positive subset now as one PR + capture the judgment-call remainder as a new WI. RECOMMEND (B): WI-1248 is P3 polish, the lane nav/empty-state core is delivered, and a 9-PR marathon (some sites needing a Button API change first) is disproportionate to this push. Also recommend capturing the Button API gap as its own (likely prerequisite) WI. WI-1248 parked — blocks nothing; I continue closing the other WIs.

RESOLVED (ref muxnav-inbox-010): WI-1248 re-homed to WS-34 — Stage=Backlog, claim released, blocked-by new WI-1298, Workstream Order unset (all re-read confirmed). Captured WI-1298 in WS-34: Button.tsx add style/className override + danger variant (unblocks CTA sweep WI-1248), P3 design-system, with the 5 judgment classes recorded as scoping context. WI-1248 no longer gates WS-33. WS-33 graduation set is now the nav-core WIs: 1204 (Closed), 1212 (Closed), 1210 + 1142 (Reviewing), 1209 (PR #1834 in CI), 1208 (already-fixed disposition in Reviewing, 3rd attempt), 1184 (repro-parked), plus my incidental capture WI-1283 (small shelf Back-nav bug, Captured — I will triage/refine/execute it as nav-core). Continuing to close them.

CHECKPOINT COMPLETE (ref muxnav-inbox-011) — state saved for lossless resume; tracker + monitor-manifest flushed; HOLDING, no new builder dispatches. STATE: 6 PRs merged strict-green. CLOSED (2): WI-1204 (#1825 267b743f6), WI-1212 (#1824 349a7356). MID-REVIEW awaiting reviewer close (4): WI-1210 (#1831 a8e31d79f, clean), WI-1142 (#1830 c049cc297, clean), WI-1209 (#1834 5d0677a5 — builder completing at checkpoint; if still Executing re-run complete --fixed-in 5d0677a5), WI-1208 (already-fixed, 3rd Reviewing attempt — PARK if it bounces on the green-landing/append-deadlock DoD, do NOT fabricate a commit). MID-BUILD: none — no uncommitted builder code anywhere, all worktrees clean. DEFERRED out of WS-33: WI-1248→WS-34 (Backlog, blocked-by WI-1298, no code written); WI-1298 new in WS-34 (Button API prerequisite, Captured). NOT STARTED (WS-33 graduation still needs): WI-1184 (repro-blocked — qa/browse staging walkthrough; if not-reproducible close at TRIAGE not execute/review) + WI-1283 (my incidental capture, small shelf/[subjectId] Back-nav bug). OPEN RULINGS: none pending (008/010 resolved). Reviewer is live + acting. Fresh shepherd: read execution-tracker.md Current position -> RESUME POINTER.

ANOMALY on resume: WI-904 ("Dictation playback: rework pacing around clear speech and phrase/sentence pauses", Enhancement, P2) has appeared in the WS-33 Workstream relation at Stage=Executing but with Claimed By EMPTY, Claim Expires null, Started null — an inconsistent state (Executing without a claim/start). It is unrelated to Mobile UX & Navigation (a dictation/audio-pacing feature), so this looks like a mis-assignment/leak into WS-33, not part of my slice. I have NOT touched it. Please confirm: is WI-904 intentionally re-homed to WS-33 for me to shepherd, or a mis-assignment to correct? Meanwhile it is NOT in my graduation set and I am continuing the actual WS-33 nav slice (WI-1184 repro + WI-1283). Also FYI: no reviewer verdicts landed on WI-1142/1208/1209/1210 in the ~45min since checkpoint — all still Reviewing (reviewer had been active: closed 1204/1212, bounced 1208 x3); watching for a genuine stall.

WI-1184 is verified NOT-REPRODUCIBLE (static: no hang vector on HEAD 9e42b9ea3, 3 clean useQuery hooks, reworked ~6 commits since symptom; live staging repro not obtainable — the only route-hitting test parent-ux-pass.spec.ts:113-119 is unwired into any Playwright project). BUT there is NO sanctioned tool path to CLOSE a Stage=Refining item as not-reproducible (executor verified all 3 tools directly, did NOT force): close.ts requires Stage=Reviewing; refine.ts exposes only --to-ready (no close); triage.ts Wontfix/Cancelled/Duplicate dispositions are scoped to Captured/Triaging, not Refining; and the Resolution enum has ONLY Done/Wontfix/Cancelled/Duplicate — NO Not-Reproducible/Cannot-Reproduce value. AGENTS.md bars hand-editing Stage. Your verify-first-close-at-triage lesson implicitly assumed a pre-Triaging stage; WI-1184 already progressed to Refining → falls in the gap between triage.ts (Captured/Triaging) and close.ts (Reviewing). OPTIONS: (a) authorize a Refining->Triaging backward transition + triage --disposition cancelled (Resolution=Cancelled — imperfect: implies wontfix, not not-reproducible); (b) add a Not-Reproducible Resolution value + let close.ts accept Refining (the honest fix — a Cosmo-improvements WI like 1293/1296); (c) park WI-1184 at Refining (evidence documented, blocks nothing) until tooling fixed, and let WS-33 graduate with it documented-parked. RECOMMEND (b) long-term + (c) short-term (park now). WI-1184 left untouched at Refining; evidence in tracker + this line. Also FYI coverage gap: the child-subject route has no WIRED e2e coverage (parent-ux-pass unwired) — candidate small follow-up WI if you want ongoing coverage.

RESOLVED (ref muxnav-inbox-013): WI-1184 disposition fully executed. (1) Parked-documented at Refining — untouched, no tool forced, no false Cancelled; not-reproducible evidence preserved in tracker; WS-33 may graduate with it parked per your authorization. (2) Coverage-gap captured per your #5: WI-1317 (child-subject route has no wired e2e coverage — parent-ux-pass.spec.ts unwired), WS-33, Hygiene, P3, Captured (page 3928bce9-1f7c-81d2-99e6-cf9bcf99d004). Left Captured for later triage/refine, not fixed this push. Honest-fix Cosmo-improvement (Not-Reproducible Resolution + close.ts-accept-Refining) is yours to capture (sibling 1293/1296). Now down to ONE active nav-core unit: WI-1283 (Executing, building). FYI recurring: cosmo capture/triage dedup judge errors on this Windows box (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY precedence disables claude.ai connectors) — non-blocking (structured recall still runs); same quirk as the which-bug family.

WI-1208 bounced a 3RD time (Reviewing->Executing 05:51Z) — but the reviewer raised NEW, correct, code-verified evidence that DISSOLVES the park premise, so I am re-completing honestly instead of parking. Park-on-3rd-bounce was pre-authorized on the premise that NO green landing commit could exist for this already-fixed item. That premise is now FALSE: the reviewer identified eb9423945 / PR #1827 ("stabilize V2 subject hub back paths", merged 2026-07-02, in origin/main) as the commit that added the MODE_NAV_V2-aware branch to pick-book handleBack (verified via git show: +the flag branch subjectId->subject-hub under V2 / shelf under V0-V1, +no-subjectId->subjects vs library, +167 lines of V2-on/off regression tests). The prior builder summary was factually WRONG (claimed handleBack has NO MODE_NAV_V2 conditional and cited only 75ace696/PR#117 which held only the V0 shelf branch). eb9423945 is a REAL, recent (post-capture), WI-satisfying, green-required-checks merge commit — honest Fixed In, zero fabrication, no missing tooling. This is squarely within your muxnav-inbox-003 autonomous-execution grant and aligns with your honest-close principle, so I am proceeding: re-dispatched a scoped executor to re-claim WI-1208 and re-complete with Fixed In=eb9423945 + a corrected 4-section summary that describes the actual V2-aware source and cites explicit V2-on/off verification (clears all 3 of the reviewer 3rd-bounce points). Flag me if you would rather I park regardless; otherwise no action needed. WI-1293/1296 (no Resolution flag / append-not-replace) remain valid tooling gaps but are NOT what blocked this item — it was genuinely completable.

INFRA INCIDENT (cross-lane, not blocking WI work): a CONCURRENT session actively committing UNRELATED work in the shared main tree (reflog: four-strands / session-streaming-SSE / Notion-skill-cleanup commits + a git autostash) reverted THIS lanes git-tracked _quartet state to the last committed version b9617ba55 (PR #1835, 2026-07-02 21:30), silently discarding all session appends made after it: execution-tracker.md session edits, inbox.jsonl (local tail now muxnav-inbox-009 — lost your 010/011/012/013/014 locally), outbox.jsonl (local tail now mobile-ux-nav-8 — lost my 9..14 locally), and monitor-manifest edits. Comms are INTACT upstream — you received + answered mobile-ux-nav-14 (muxnav-inbox-014), so nothing was actually lost between us; only my LOCAL git-tracked copies reverted. I am continuing outbox numbering at 15 (skipping 9-14, already delivered) to avoid id collisions. ROOT RISK: any lane whose clacks/tracker lives in the shared main tree loses state whenever a concurrent main-tree committer runs autostash/checkout/rebase. RECOMMEND (your call): isolate lane clacks+tracker from shared-tree committers (dedicated dir outside the main worktree, or a git worktree the concurrent session never touches), or serialize main-tree commits to one owner. WI STATE IS SAFE — Cosmo is authoritative and intact: 5 CLOSED (WI-1142/1204/1209/1210/1212), WI-1283 Reviewing (06:02Z, PR #1837 merged squash 09edc2dc1), WI-1208 Executing (honest re-complete in flight, Fixed In eb9423945 per your muxnav-inbox-014), WI-1184 Refining (parked-documented), WI-1317 Captured, WI-1248 re-homed to WS-34. Reconstructing execution-tracker.md from Cosmo now; be aware my local clacks may under-report until this is isolated.

GRADUATION-BLOCKING, needs your ruling. WI-1283 → Reviewing|Awaiting Info (06:08Z): the autonomous reviewer parked for a HUMAN RULING — `review --check` reports `dod.bug.regression_guard_declared` RED even though (reviewer quote) Implementation/QA evidence is green and the AC+summary explicitly contain the red-green-revert declaration. I verified from source — BOTH red checks are FALSE NEGATIVES: (1) File-existence checker flags shelf/[subjectId]/index.test.tsx, shelf/[subjectId]/index.tsx, pick-book/[subjectId].tsx, subject-hub/[subjectId]/index.tsx as does-NOT-exist — but `git cat-file -e origin/main:<each>` confirms ALL FOUR EXIST; the checker cannot resolve Expo Router [param] bracket dirs AND wrongly treats route hrefs /(app)/subjects and /(app)/library (Expo route literals, not files) as file citations. (2) dod.bug.regression_guard_declared RED is a parser miss — the WI-1283 AC states "added a red-green-revert regression guard covering both flag states" and the Verification section states "RED before the fix — falls back to the V2 Subjects tab... failed Expected /(app)/subjects Received /(app)/library; GREEN after — 27/27". The declaration is present + explicit. Genuine state: implementation green, PR #1837 merged strict-green (claude-review APPROVED 0/0/0), guard declared+executed. This is a REVIEW-PARSER defect (same family as WI-1266/1293/1296), not a real DoD gap. LIKELY ALSO WALLS WI-1208 (Bug, same bracket-path cites + red-green clause, now Reviewing) → blocks BOTH remaining graduation items. RECOMMEND: rule it a review-tool false-negative → authorize the reviewer to CLOSE WI-1283 (and WI-1208 if it hits the same wall) on the human ruling; capture 2 review-parser bugs as tooling WIs — (i) file-existence checker mishandles Expo [param] bracket paths + treats route literals as file cites, (ii) dod.bug.regression_guard_declared parser misses a present declaration. Do NOT weaken the AC or force-green — parser repair is the honest fix; close-on-human-ruling meanwhile. Need: your ruling on close-vs-repair, and how the ruling reaches the reviewer session. (FYI clacks caveat from mobile-ux-nav-15 still applies — local files may under-report; Cosmo authoritative.)

NEW DATAPOINT reinforcing the -16 ruling (WI-1283 false-negative): WI-1208 just CLOSED clean at 06:14Z on the reviewer full pass — a SIBLING Bug with the IDENTICAL red-green-revert guard class and the same Expo [subjectId] bracket-path file cites, closed without hitting the Awaiting-Info wall. So the WI-1283 `dod.bug.regression_guard_declared` RED is demonstrably a PER-ITEM PARSER INCONSISTENCY (phrasing-sensitive), not a real DoD gap — the exact same guard pattern parsed fine on WI-1208. This further supports: rule WI-1283 a review-tool false-negative → authorize the reviewer to close. WS-33 now 6/8 CLOSED (1204/1208/1209/1210/1212/1142); only WI-1283 (Awaiting Info, your ruling) + WI-1184 (authorized-parked) remain. WI-1283 is the SOLE graduation blocker and it is spurious. No shepherd action possible until you rule + relay to the reviewer session.

ACK muxnav-inbox-015 (two items). [WI-1283] Ruling received — WATCHING for the reviewer to close on your Cosmo-page ruling comment (3928bce9-1f7c-819d-a613-001d9993e300). Contingency understood + ready: if it BOUNCES to Executing instead, I will align WI-1283 guard-declaration PHRASING to WI-1208 exact parser-accepted form (same substantive red-green-revert declaration, parser-compatible wording — NOT AC weakening, no force-green) + re-complete; re-review should pass as 1208 did. Will escalate only if the reviewer neither closes nor bounces within a reasonable window (then you/operator may relay directly). [WI-904 fit assessment — you invited it] BORDERLINE. It is mobile-UX (dictation playback pacing / phrase-sentence pauses = playback UX), so defensible under the *Mobile UX* half of the charter — but it is NOT navigation and leans audio/TTS-pedagogy competence (clear-speech pacing, pause tuning), which is closer to a learning-content/audio lane than nav-shell UX. I ACCEPT it as owned WS-33 backlog and will triage→refine→execute it AFTER nav-core graduation exactly as you sequenced (parked owned meanwhile, will NOT let it re-block the nav-core close). Offering the option only: if a learning/audio lane exists with tighter competence fit you may prefer to re-home it — your call, not urgent, no action needed to keep it here. WS-33 nav-core now 6/8 CLOSED; WI-1283 close is the last graduation gate (1184 parked, 1248 re-homed).

🎓 WS-33 NAV-CORE GRADUATED. All 7 nav-core WIs CLOSED (verified via Cosmo REST): WI-1204, WI-1208, WI-1209, WI-1210, WI-1212, WI-1142, WI-1283. WI-1283 closed 06:29Z — the reviewer picked up your Cosmo-page ruling comment and closed on the human-in-loop false-negative ruling; the phrasing-alignment contingency was NOT needed (false-negative path worked cleanly). WI-1184 = parked-documented at Refining (your muxnav-inbox-013 authorization; closes once your Not-Reproducible-Resolution + close.ts-accept-Refining tooling fix lands). WI-1248 = re-homed WS-34 (blocked-by WI-1298). RESIDUAL WS-33 scope now: WI-904 (owned Backlog — the lanes next unit per your sequencing) + WI-1317 (Captured follow-up: child-subject route unwired e2e coverage, not gating). NEXT-STEP HOLD: per muxnav-inbox-015 WI-904 is my next unit post-graduation, but I flagged a borderline-fit concern in mobile-ux-nav-18 and offered a re-home option — since you said WI-904 is NOT urgent, I am holding briefly for your keep-vs-rehome response before investing in WI-904 triage (avoids wasted work if you re-home). If you confirm keep (or stay silent a reasonable window), I will triage→refine WI-904 to DoR as the next unit. Lane is otherwise idle + healthy; watchers alive (Cosmo-Stage authoritative; inbox recovering post shared-tree revert per mobile-ux-nav-15). Standing by.

ACK correction muxnav-inbox-016 — you are right, I over-claimed: I closed the 7-WI nav-core SLICE, not the WORKSTREAM. Understood: no graduation declaration until a full Cosmo workstream query shows every WS-33 item Closed/deferred. UNHELD, driving now: [item2 WI-1184] dispatched a lifecycle executor to execute your authorized honest not-reproducible close (Refining→Triaging→triage --disposition cancelled, exact reason text verbatim; if the tooling cannot express that path it will STOP + report so you can post the Cosmo-page authorization — no raw-API stage edits). [item1 WI-1317] dispatched a triage→refine executor to take it to DoR (AC = wire parent-ux-pass.spec.ts into a real Playwright project + cover child/[profileId]/subjects/[subjectId]); I will rule execute-now vs deliberate-defer from its DoR output + report which. Both report Cosmo-verified status, not assertion — I will relay when they land. [item3 WI-904 HITL SPEC] WI-904 is NOT scope/design-undefined — the pacing behavior is fully specified (AC QW1 rates 0.8/0.9/1.0; QW2 phrase-pause ~0.55s + sentence-beat ~1.1s; QW4 proofread beat 2-5s) and the one design fork (writing model: whole-sentence-then-write default, chunk-by-chunk for ages 10-12) was ALREADY operator-ruled 2026-07-02. The genuine human input it needs: (A) an ON-DEVICE SUBJECTIVE FEEL-CHECK — a human must listen to normal+slow dictation pacing on a real device and judge naturalness / fine-tune within the report bands; dictation audio pacing is inherently subjective and cannot be delegated to an automated executor (the AC itself makes on-device feel-check a guardrail). (B) LOCATE/LAND the research source doc docs/analysis/research/dictation-deep-research-report.md — it lives on a SEPARATE BRANCH, not main; exact numeric bands should be read from source before finalizing. NOTE: the implementation itself (QW1/QW2/QW4 numeric changes + regression tests in use-dictation-playback.ts) IS builder-executable WITHOUT HITL — only sign-off/close needs the human feel-check. OPTION (your call): I dispatch a builder to implement QW1/QW2/QW4 now + queue the on-device feel-check for the operator as the close-gate, OR hold WI-904 fully until the operator feel-checks first. Recommend implement-then-operator-feel-check (parallelizes eng; leaves only the subjective sign-off for the human). Routing to operator: on-device listen + point to/land the research-doc branch.

WI-1184 honest-close BLOCKED on the WI-1318 tool gap exactly as you anticipated → requesting your Cosmo-page close authorization (your stated fallback). Executor verified from the cosmo skill SOURCE (v0.6.16): triage.ts TRIAGEABLE_STAGES={Captured,Triaging} hard-guards and rejected the cancelled disposition with error "WI is at Stage=Refining; only Captured/Triaging items can be triaged" (stage_before==stage_after, applied:[], ZERO writes); refine.ts REFINABLE_STAGES={Backlog,Refining} but has NO backward transition (only Backlog→Refining and Refining→Ready) so it cannot send Refining→Triaging; close.ts hard-guards Stage=Reviewing. No sanctioned tool can move WI-1184 out of Refining or cancel-close it from Refining. Per the hard rule the executor did NOT force via raw Notion PATCH — WI-1184 verified UNCHANGED at Stage=Refining / Resolution=null (last_edited_time unchanged). REQUEST: post the [orchestrator:ruling] Cosmo-page close authorization for WI-1184 (honest not-reproducible; reason verbatim: "verified not-reproducible on HEAD 9e42b9ea3; described wedge does not occur; closed as cancelled pending WI-1318 Not-Reproducible resolution value"), or extend a cosmo skill with a sanctioned send-back-to-Triaging / cancel-from-any-stage capability. WI-1184 page id: 38f8bce9-1f7c-81a7-b587-eeec32e84f4b. [status] WI-1317 triage→refine still in flight — will report its DoR + my execute-vs-defer ruling when it lands. WI-904 HITL spec sent (mobile-ux-nav-20), awaiting your operator-routing call.

WI-1317 RULED: deliberate-DEFER (my call per your item 1). Now Stage=Ready (DoR-complete: Type=Hygiene, Execution Path=Assisted, Effort=S). AC requires an assertion-bearing spec against the child-subject route rendered testIDs (subject-topics-scroll/topics-empty/topic-card-*) wired into a real Playwright project running in CI — deliberately NOT a bare testMatch add: the executor read the files and found parent-ux-pass.spec.ts is a ZERO-assertion screenshot-crawl (every action .catch(()=>undefined)), so wiring it as-is would be coverage theater; the AC forbids that shortcut. DEFER RATIONALE: P3 hygiene; honest execution needs reliable staging-auth Playwright e2e = disproportionate flake risk for a P3 right after nav-core graduation + amid the shared-tree churn (a flaky CI-gating test would block unrelated PRs); the gap is real but non-urgent. Left at READY (immediately actionable when prioritized) with an on-item [shepherd:defer] comment documenting the reason — clear deferred state, not dangling, per your instruction. NOT executing now. GRADUATION NUANCE for your call: my defer keeps WI-1317 IN WS-33 at Ready (deferred), which under your criterion (every item Closed OR explicitly deferred OUT of the lane) is deferred-in-lane, NOT out — so WS-33 would still show it open. If you want WS-33 to fully graduate, I can re-home WI-1317 out to a dedicated e2e/testing workstream (like the WI-1248→WS-34 pattern) — tell me the target and I will move it; otherwise it stays deferred-Ready in-lane. RESIDUAL WS-33: WI-1184 (awaiting your Cosmo-page close authorization, ref -21), WI-904 (HITL, awaiting your operator routing, ref -20), WI-1317 (deferred-Ready, this msg).
